claim: YOLO evidence dispatch boundary audit already covers git ls-files tracked/cached/other files, fallback walks for non-git projects, and excludes only the dispatch artifact root. The implementation detects tracked, untracked, git-ignored, and symlink mutations in both git-backed and non-git projects.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot merges git ls-files results with walkFiles fallback and excludes allowedRoots (artifact directory)
evidence.why: This is the core boundary snapshot logic that combines git-tracked files with fallback discovery.

evidence.summary: gitFiles runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' which returns cached, others, and exclude-standard files
evidence.why: The git command covers tracked, cached, untracked, and non-excluded files including gitignored entries outside standard exclusions.

evidence.summary: walkFiles recursively walks directories and excludes .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: This is the fallback for non-git projects and supplements git ls-files results.

evidence.summary: boundaryEntryDigest returns sha256 hash for files, symlink target for symlinks, and mode:size:mtime for other nodes
evidence.why: This creates cryptographic digests that detect mutations including symlink additions.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after and detects added, modified, and deleted files
evidence.why: This is the change detection logic that identifies mutations.

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundary snapshots before and after agent execution then diffs them
evidence.why: This shows the boundary audit is integrated into the dispatch runtime execution flow.

evidence.summary: Test confirms git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations
evidence.why: Evidence that untracked file detection works in git-backed projects.

evidence.summary: Test confirms git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs
evidence.why: Evidence that gitignored file detection works when outside standard exclusions.

evidence.summary: Test confirms non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts
evidence.why: Evidence that non-git fallback walks detect modifications while excluding artifact roots.

evidence.summary: Test confirms non-git boundary fallback detects added symlink entries
evidence.why: Evidence that symlink detection works in non-git projects.

evidence.summary: Test confirms boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary violations block readiness regardless of agent results.

evidence.summary: Test confirms boundary mutation probe prompts a controlled write and blocks readiness
evidence.why: Evidence that stress testing with mutation probes works and is detected as violations.

risks: If claim is wrong, boundary audit could miss mutations in certain file types or edge cases, leading to undetected target project mutations.

result.notes: Boundary audit implementation is complete with tests covering git-backed (tracked/untracked/gitignored) and non-git fallback scenarios. The demand scope_in and desired_outcome are already satisfied by existing code and test evidence.
